---
title: "Credit Data Modeling 2 - XgBoost & Random Forest"
output: html_document
date: "2025-09-08"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
library(foreign)
library(car)
library(carData)
library(zoo)
library(lmtest)
library(dynlm)
library(sandwich)
library(stargazer)
library(dplyr)
library(systemfit)
library(caTools)
library(readxl)
library(stats)
library(AER)
library(stringr)
library(mlogit)
library(nnet)
library(caret)
library(reshape2)
library(survival)
library(xts)
library(forecast)
library(Metrics)
library(tseries)
library(tsutils)
library(ggplot2)
library(urca)
library(rugarch)
library(Information)
library(readr)
```

```{r}
credit = read_csv("E:/NEU SLIDE/DATA SCIENCE/credit_data_scaled.csv")
```

Information Value
```{r}
library(scorecard)
# Chuyển đổi biến mục tiêu thành 0/1
dt <- credit
dt$y <- ifelse(dt$loan_status_bin == "Bad", 1, 0)
dt$loan_status_bin <- NULL
dt$y <- as.numeric(dt$y)

# Tính IV
iv_list <- iv(dt, y = "y")

head(iv_list)

# Lọc biến có IV >= 0.02
iv_selected <- subset(iv_list, info_value >= 0.02)

# Sắp xếp theo IV giảm dần
iv_selected <- iv_selected[order(-iv_selected$info_value), ]

# Xem top 10 biến quan trọng
head(iv_selected, 10)
```


```{r}
# Lấy danh sách tên biến
selected_vars <- iv_selected$variable
```


```{r}
library(caret)
library(pROC)

# Chuẩn hóa biến mục tiêu
credit$y <- ifelse(credit$loan_status_bin == "Bad", 1, 0)
credit$y <- as.numeric(credit$y)

# Train/Test split
set.seed(123)
train_index <- createDataPartition(credit$y, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train_data <- credit[train_index, ]
test_data  <- credit[-train_index, ]
```

1. Xgboost

```{r}
library(xgboost)
# XGBoost matrix
train_matrix <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data = as.matrix(train_data[, selected_vars]),
  label = train_data$y
)
test_matrix <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data = as.matrix(test_data[, selected_vars]),
  label = test_data$y
)

```





```{r}
# Thiết lập tham số
params <- list(
  objective = "binary:logistic",
  eval_metric = "auc",
  max_depth = 6,
  eta = 0.1,
  subsample = 0.8,
  colsample_bytree = 0.8
)
# Huấn luyện mô hình
xgb_model <- xgb.train(params, train_matrix, nrounds = 100, watchlist = list(train = train_matrix, eval = test_matrix), early_stopping_rounds = 10, verbose = 0)
```


```{r}
# Dự đoán trên tập test
test_data$pred_prob <- predict(xgb_model, newdata = test_matrix)
test_data$pred_class <- ifelse(test_data$pred_prob >= 0.5, "Bad", "Good")

#ROC và AUC
roc_obj <- roc(test_data$loan_status_bin, test_data$pred_prob, levels = c("Good", "Bad"), direction = "<")
auc_val <- auc(roc_obj)
gini_val <- 2 * auc_val - 1
cat("AUC :", auc_val, "\n")
cat("Gini:", gini_val, "\n")
plot(roc_obj, col = "blue", main = "ROC Curve - XGBoost")

```

2. Random Forest

```{r}
library(randomForest)
# Huấn luyện mô hình Random Forest
rf_model <- randomForest(as.factor(y) ~ ., data = train_data[, c(selected_vars, "y")], ntree = 20, mtry = 5, importance = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# Dự đoán trên tập test
test_data$pred_prob_rf <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data[, selected_vars], type = "prob")[,2]
test_data$pred_class_rf <- ifelse(test_data$pred_prob_rf >= 0.5, "Bad", "Good")
#ROC và AUC
roc_obj_rf <- roc(test_data$loan_status_bin, test_data$pred_prob_rf, levels = c("Good", "Bad"), direction = "<")
auc_val_rf <- auc(roc_obj_rf)
gini_val_rf <- 2 * auc_val_rf - 1
cat("AUC RF :", auc_val_rf, "\n")
cat("Gini RF:", gini_val_rf, "\n")
plot(roc_obj_rf, col = "green", main = "ROC Curve - Random Forest")

```

```{r}
library(ranger)

rf_model <- ranger(
  formula = as.factor(y) ~ ., 
  data = train_data[, c(selected_vars, "y")], 
  num.trees = 100, 
  mtry = 5,
  importance = "impurity",
  probability = TRUE
)


```

```{r}
# Dự đoán trên tập test
test_data$pred_prob_ranger <- predict(rf_model, data = test_data[, selected_vars])$predictions[,2]
test_data$pred_class_ranger <- ifelse(test_data$pred_prob_ranger >= 0.5, "Bad", "Good")
#ROC và AUC
roc_obj_ranger <- roc(test_data$loan_status_bin, test_data$pred_prob_ranger, levels = c("Good", "Bad"), direction = "<")
auc_val_ranger <- auc(roc_obj_ranger)
gini_val_ranger <- 2 * auc_val_ranger - 1
cat("AUC Ranger :", auc_val_ranger, "\n")
cat("Gini Ranger:", gini_val_ranger, "\n")
plot(roc_obj_ranger, col = "purple", main = "ROC Curve - Ranger")

```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
# Biểu đồ tầm quan trọng biến
importance_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(rf_model$variable.importance),
  Importance = rf_model$variable.importance
)

importance_df <- importance_df[order(-importance_df$Importance), ]

ggplot(head(importance_df, 10), aes(x = reorder(Variable, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top 10 Important Variables - Ranger RF",
       x = "Variables", y = "Importance")


```

```{r}
library(caret)

confusionMatrix(
  as.factor(test_data$pred_class_ranger),
  as.factor(test_data$loan_status_bin),
  positive = "Bad"
)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

calib_df <- data.frame(
  pred_prob = test_data$pred_prob_ranger,
  actual = ifelse(test_data$loan_status_bin == "Bad", 1, 0)
)

calib_df$bin <- cut(calib_df$pred_prob, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1), include.lowest = TRUE)
calib_summary <- aggregate(actual ~ bin, data = calib_df, FUN = mean)
calib_summary$mid <- seq(0.05, 0.95, 0.1)

ggplot(calib_summary, aes(x = mid, y = actual)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Calibration Plot - Random Forest",
       x = "Predicted Probability",
       y = "Observed Frequency")

```
